# Code of Conduct

## Our Commitment

We are committed to providing a welcoming, inclusive, and respectful environment for all contributors and community members, regardless of age, body size, disability, ethnicity, gender identity and expression, level of experience, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, sex, sexual identity and orientation, or socioeconomic status.

---

## Our Standards

Examples of behavior that contributes to creating a positive environment include:

- **Being respectful** — Treat all community members with dignity and respect
- **Being inclusive** — Welcome people of all backgrounds and experiences
- **Being collaborative** — Work together constructively toward shared goals
- **Being empathetic** — Seek to understand different perspectives and experiences
- **Being professional** — Maintain professional communication in all interactions
- **Giving constructive feedback** — Provide helpful criticism with intent to improve
- **Taking responsibility** — Acknowledge mistakes and work to improve

Examples of unacceptable behavior include:

- Harassment, intimidation, or discrimination based on any protected characteristic
- Offensive comments or slurs (including those targeting gender, religion, or other identities)
- Unwelcome sexual attention or advances
- Deliberate intimidation, stalking, or following
- Trolling, insulting/derogatory comments, or personal attacks
- Publishing others' private information (doxxing)
- Any form of violence or threats of violence
- Sustained disruption of discussions or events

---

## Enforcement Responsibilities

Community leaders are responsible for clarifying and enforcing our standards of acceptable behavior and will take appropriate corrective action in response to any behavior they determine to be inappropriate, threatening, offensive, or harmful.

---

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies to:

- All project spaces (GitHub, forums, documentation, etc.)
- Official community events and gatherings
- All contributors and maintainers
- Public representations of the project

This Code of Conduct also applies outside project spaces when an individual's statements or actions have a direct impact on the project community.

---

## Enforcement

### Reporting a Violation

If you witness or experience a Code of Conduct violation, please report it by emailing **conduct@foundry.com** with:

- Description of the incident
- Any relevant context or background
- Names/usernames involved (if applicable)
- Your contact information
- Any other relevant details

**All reports will be handled confidentially.**

### Investigation Process

1. **Acknowledgment** — We will acknowledge receipt of your report within 24 hours
2. **Investigation** — We will investigate the report thoroughly and impartially
3. **Assessment** — We will determine whether a violation occurred
4. **Resolution** — We will take appropriate corrective action
5. **Notification** — We will notify the reporter and involved parties of the outcome

### Consequences

Consequences for violations depend on severity and may include:

- Verbal or written warning
- Temporary restriction from participation
- Permanent removal from the project
- Reporting to GitHub or other platforms as appropriate

We aim to be fair, proportionate, and restorative where possible.

---

## Appeal Process

If you believe an enforcement action was incorrect or unfair, you may appeal by:

1. Emailing the appeal to **conduct@foundry.com**
2. Providing your reason for the appeal
3. Providing any additional context or evidence

Appeals will be reviewed by a different member of the maintainer team.

---

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org), version 2.0, available at https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/0/code_of_conduct.html

---

## Questions?

If you have questions about this Code of Conduct, please contact:

- **Email**: conduct@foundry.com
- **GitHub Issues**: Tag with `code-of-conduct` label

---

## Values We Uphold

### Respect
We respect different viewpoints and approaches. Disagreement is natural and can lead to better outcomes when handled respectfully.

### Inclusivity
We actively work to include people from underrepresented groups. We oppose gatekeeping and believe open-source thrives with diverse perspectives.

### Transparency
We communicate openly about decisions, conflicts, and outcomes.

### Accountability
We hold ourselves accountable to this Code of Conduct and take violations seriously.

### Growth
We believe in supporting each other's growth as developers and community members.

---

Thank you for helping make the Laravel Core community welcoming and safe for everyone.
